ELPAC Level 1
Lexile 300–650L
DTR | Listen to a Speaker Support an Opinion
LSO-MS8-L1-300650-MSELA8-CPO-GBL-02 | MS English/ELA: Grade 8 | Developing a Topic with Well-Chosen Facts and Details | Court of Public Opinion | Global Perspectives
Facts, Not Feelings
Click 🎧 to listen.
Listen to a conversation between two students. The boy is discussing his opinion about the school's global classroom program. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer four questions about the conversation and the evidence the boy gives to support his opinion.
Listen to the Conversation
1 QUESTION 1:
Why does the boy say that "it's fun" is not a good enough reason to keep the program?
2 QUESTION 2:
Where did the boy get the number he plans to lead with?
3 QUESTION 3:
What specific example does the boy give to show that the program works?
4 QUESTION 4:
What does the boy mean when he says you cannot get a citizenship skill from a textbook?
Answer all 4 questions to submit.
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Find the part where the boy says why a weak reason is not enough. Then find the part where he tells where his number came from.
CA ELD: PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.7, PI.B.8, PII.A.1 | CCSS: W.8.2.B | MS English/ELA: Grade 8
